title: Request information from the Canada Revenue Agency - Canada.ca
content:
## Request information from the Canada Revenue Agency

## The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) is updating its process for handling representatives’ access to information and privacy (ATIP) requests
Changes to access to information and privacy requests at the Canada Revenue Agency September 10
You can get many types of information from the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA), including copies of your tax slips, business information returns and the CRA’s policy manuals.
There are two steps to find the information you’re looking for.
Step 1 – Find out if the information you want is already available through CRA services. This method is usually the fastest way to get the information.
Step 2 – If you can’t find the information you’re looking for, make an access to information or personal information request. This process may take at least 30 days. Access to information requests cost $5.
- Step 1 – Find out if the information is already available

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
